title: <div>  3.  How do we want to handle grading flexibility between pathways? Should
content:
3. How do we want to handle grading flexibility between pathways? Should each path have equal weight but slightly different grading rubrics?
4. Is there any final way we want to encourage students to experiment outside their chosen path? (E.g., once per semester, they can “switch” to another path for a week).

🚀 Once these questions are answered, we are fully locked in!

📌 Revised Course Protocols Based on Discussion & Feedback


I’ve now incorporated the Catharsis framework, refined the student paths, adjusted the Mastery Exam Challenge, built out the Final Reflection Letter, and am addressing grading flexibility and cross-path opportunities. Below is the fully revised plan.

1️⃣ Catharsis Discussion Threads (Expanded & Reworked)

The Catharsis Discussion now has two parts every week:
1. 🎭 Pre-Class Catharsis (Before Lecture, No Readings Required)
• Encourages engagement with thematic ideas that already exist in culture.
context: From document: Comprehensive Course Structure and Alternating Schedule (A & B Weeks… — chunk 49 (chunk 49/108)
